_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Ureteric sampling including cystoscopy, ureteric catheterization, retrograde pyelogram
clinical_indications: Ureteral occlusion for injection of contrast media
Delivery of irrigation fluids to the ureter
Drainage of fluids from the urinary tract
Access, advancement or exchange of ancillary devices including guidewires
Non-visualization of ureteral segment on intravenous pyelography and CT Urography
Better characterization of ureteral or pelvicalyceal abnormalities seen on IVU or CTU
Access for brush biopsies of a suspicious area of urothelium
Evaluation of placement of a catheter or a ureteral stent

preauth.label: Clinical notes including evaluation findings and planned line of treatment

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes include evaluation findings and planned line of treatment?
X-ray/USG/CT Scan report includes patient ID and date?
Clinical notes and X-ray/USG/CT Scan report indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Detailed Indoor Case Papers contain daily vitals and treatment details?

common_queries: Clinical notes or X-ray/USG/CT Scan report not indicative of the Ureteric sampling procedure.
Clinical notes missing evaluation findings or planned line of treatment.
X-ray/USG/CT Scan report missing patient ID or date.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers lacking daily vitals or complete treatment details.
Histopathology report not submitted at claim stage.
Detailed discharge summary not submitted at claim stage.

clinical_key_pointers: Cystoscopy: Cystoscopy to visualize the urethral mucosa, ectopic ureters, the prostatic urethra, the area of the trigone where urine can be seen entering through the ureters, and the bladder mucosa.
Ureteric catheterization: Ureteric catheterization is inserted into the ureter to prevent or treat obstruction of the urine flow from the kidney.

Retrograde Pyelogram: A retrograde pyelogram is a type of X-ray that allows visualization of the bladder, ureters, and renal pelvis, is performed during the cystoscopy, evaluation of the bladder with an endoscope. During a cystoscopy, contrast dye, which helps enhance the X-ray images, can be introduced into the ureters via a catheter.
